name: Precious Metals Refinery
description_short: Precious Metals Refinery buys gold, jewelry, watches, and coins at locations in Phoenix, Tucson, and California. They offer free evaluations, repairs, and custom design services.
description_long:
Precious Metals Refinery (PMR) is a precious metals and luxury goods buyer operating retail locations in Arizona and California. The company specializes in purchasing gold, silver, platinum, jewelry, watches, coins, and diamonds directly from consumers. Their Phoenix location on Indian School Road is managed by Olga Cottone and operates Monday through Saturday, 10 AM to 6 PM. The company markets itself as a trusted alternative to traditional pawn shops, emphasizing transparent pricing and expert evaluation.

PMR's core business model centers on buying precious metals and collectibles from individuals. They claim to offer competitive payouts backed by GIA-certified gemologists, numismatists, and watch specialists. The company evaluates items in-person at their retail locations using state-of-the-art equipment. Beyond buying, PMR advertises in-home consultations for large estates, jewelry and watch repair services, and custom jewelry design. They position themselves as full-service precious metals brokers rather than traditional pawn lenders.

PMR differentiates itself through several operational claims: transparent in-person evaluation with customers present during assessment, price-matching guarantees against other Phoenix-area buyers, GIA-certified staff, luxury brand expertise (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe, Tiffany & Co.), and private buying offices. They emphasize confidentiality and security in transactions. The website does not clearly state whether they offer loans against items or only outright purchases, which is a key distinction from traditional pawn operations.

As a precious metals buyer, PMR serves consumers liquidating inherited jewelry, estate collections, or personal items, but functions as a retail buyer rather than a credit or lending solution. The main limitation is that this is a buyer service, not a lender—customers receive cash for items but do not access credit products. The business model depends entirely on commodity prices and buyer demand, creating potential valuation disputes despite claims of transparency.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: GIA-certified gemologists and watch specialists on staff provide professional appraisals
Free, no-pressure evaluations with no obligation to sell
Transparent in-person buying process with customers present during assessment
In-home consultation service available for large estates and high-value collections
Full-service jewelry and watch repair capabilities in-house
Price-matching guarantee—claims to beat written offers from other Scottsdale, Tempe, and Phoenix buyers
Custom jewelry design and estate collection services
Multiple convenient locations in Arizona and California with extended hours (open Saturday)
cons: Operates as a buyer/seller of precious metals only—not a lending service, so no credit or loan access despite pawn-shop categorization
Payouts entirely dependent on commodity market prices and company's own valuations; no price transparency published on website
No clear disclosure of whether they offer collateral-based loans (traditional pawn function) or only outright purchases
Price-matching guarantee limited to specific geographic area (Phoenix/Scottsdale/Tempe) and requires written competing offers
Valuation disputes possible despite 'transparent' claims; consumer has limited recourse if they disagree with appraised value
best_for: Individuals with inherited jewelry, watches, or estate collections they want to liquidate
People selling luxury watches or designer jewelry from brands like Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe, or Tiffany & Co.
Those with large precious metals collections seeking in-home professional appraisals
Consumers needing jewelry repair, cleaning, or custom design services while liquidating other items
diagnosis: Precious Metals Refinery is best for individuals liquidating precious metals, luxury watches, or inherited jewelry collections. However, note that this is a commodity buyer, not a lending service—customers exchange items for cash rather than obtaining credit, making it fundamentally different from traditional pawn shops that loan against collateral.
services: Gold, silver, platinum, and coin buying
Jewelry purchase (engagement rings, designer pieces, estate collections)
Luxury watch buying and appraisal (Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Breitling, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe)
Free in-person evaluations using industry-standard tools
In-home consultations for large collections and high-value items
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 4340 E Indian School Rd #5, Phoenix, AZ 85018
